name: Tierra del Fuego (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the snow and ice of Antarctica and the southern austral lands
the 'land of fire' from which the province takes its name and the flight of the albatross
the South Atlantic Ocean and the southern sky

description: A white field crossed diagonally by a stylised orange band suggesting the flight of an albatross and a blue band charged with the five white stars of the Southern Cross constellation.
history: Adopted by provincial law 207 on 26 June 1999 after a public competition won by Hipolito Mayorga. The orange shape represents both the flame for which the territory was named (Magellan's 'land of fire') and the flight of the albatross; the blue band bears the Southern Cross constellation, a reminder of the province's far-southern position.

features.symbolism: a stylised composition: a white field crossed diagonally by an orange band evoking flame and an albatross's flight, and a blue band charged with the Southern Cross, alluding to the province's full name 'Tierra del Fuego, Antartida e Islas del Atlantico Sur'
the Crux constellation visible in the southern sky and a recurring symbol of the southern hemisphere
